title: An AI Assistant Nurturing Tool
description: MulmoClaude's vision: an AI assistant is not a finished product you buy, but something you nurture. This deck explains why an assistant needs a growth environment, why service-provider lock-in matters, and how MulmoClaude lets people grow an assistant on their own computer.

beats.text: Last week, I wrote that I was still trying to find the right words for what MulmoClaude is. I think I have found them: an AI assistant nurturing tool. An AI assistant is not a finished product you buy. It is something you nurture for yourself.

slide.title: An **AI Assistant** Nurturing Tool
slide.subtitle: An AI assistant is not something you {warning:buy}. It is something you {primary:nurture}.
slide.author: Satoshi Nakajima
chips: An AI beside you all day

beats.text: By AI assistant, I mean an AI that stays with us all day and helps across work and everyday life. That is different from an AI agent, which performs a specific task on a person's behalf. In human terms, an AI assistant is closer to a secretary or a butler. An AI agent is closer to a lawyer, a cook, or a gardener.

slide.title: An assistant is not an agent

items: Helps broadly across work and life
Closer to a secretary or butler
Closer to a lawyer, cook, or gardener

beats.text: When people hear AI assistant, many picture smart glasses. But smart glasses are only one device for accessing the assistant. A phone, a watch, and a desktop are also doorways. The assistant itself should not be trapped inside one particular device.

slide.title: The assistant is not the device
slide.footer: What matters is reaching the same assistant from every doorway.

beats.text: There is another important point. The substance of an AI assistant is not the AI model itself, such as GPT or Claude. The model is the engine. To do good work, the assistant has to know the person well: past conversations, calendar, contacts, to-do lists, email, meeting notes, journals, and even the apps that person uses.

slide.quote: The substance of an AI assistant is not the AI model itself.
slide.author: The model is the engine
slide.role: What matters is the accumulation that lets it understand the person

beats.text: It makes sense that OpenAI and Anthropic are investing in memory. Meta and Google, which already hold a great deal of user data, are looking at the same place. The value of an AI assistant is not only in the model. It is in the data and applications accumulated around one person.

slide.title: The accumulation that makes an assistant smart

items.title: Data
items.description: Calendar, contacts, to-dos, email, notes, journals.

items.title: Apps
items.description: Tools the person uses, or creates when needed.

slide.footer: Whoever controls this accumulation controls the assistant.

beats.text: But I do not think it is wise to entrust something as important as an AI assistant to a single service provider, especially when it will keep accumulating your personal data and applications. The longer you use it, the more painful switching becomes. Eventually, you may find yourself unable to leave that service at all.

slide.quote: The longer you use it, the more painful switching becomes.
slide.author: The risk of depending on one service
slide.role: If someone else owns the accumulation, they own the assistant

beats.text: That is the reason I began building MulmoClaude: to make it possible to grow an AI assistant while keeping its data, applications, and memory under the user's control. But control does not mean buying a finished assistant. An AI assistant is something you nurture. When you first install MulmoClaude, it has no accumulated data and no personal applications. As it stands, it cannot yet play the role of your AI assistant.

slide.quote: The assistant you depend on should be one you can {primary:nurture and control}.
slide.author: The starting point of MulmoClaude
slide.role: It is fine to start empty. You grow it from there.

beats.text: To nurture an assistant, you need an environment. A place where memory can accumulate. A place to keep data. A place to create personal applications. A place where, through natural language conversation, you can keep adding the tools you need. MulmoClaude is my attempt to create a growth environment for an AI assistant.

slide.title: A growth environment for an AI assistant

items.title: Keep data
items.description: Hold the records of your work and life.

items.title: Grow apps
items.description: Create personal tools as needs arise.

slide.footer: The harness is part of this environment. The point is the place where the assistant can grow.

beats.text: With MulmoClaude, you can nurture an AI assistant on your own computer. Its substance accumulates there. The data, the applications, and the memory are yours. If you use GitHub for backup and version control, you can make that foundation even safer.

slide.title: Grow it on your computer, not inside a service

items: Leaving gets more painful over time
Backup and version control are possible

beats.text: Inside MulmoClaude, you talk with the AI in natural language, create applications for yourself, and accumulate many kinds of data. I think that is the right way to grow an AI assistant. A restaurant list, an invoice tracker, a vocabulary practice tool. Each one is a small application for one person.

slide.title: From conversation to personal apps
slide.footer: Add the tools you need through conversation, without writing code.

beats.text: Of course, an assistant should not only be usable when you are sitting in front of your computer. With MulmoClaude, if you log in with the same ID from both your computer and your phone, you can communicate with the AI assistant living on your computer even when you are away from home. The server only relays the request. It does not store your data or applications. That is how you can safely reach the same assistant from outside. In the future, smart glasses and smart headsets can become the same kind of doorway.

slide.title: Reach your home-computer assistant from outside

items.title: Log in with the same ID
items.description: Connect your computer and phone as the same user.

items.title: The server only relays
items.description: It carries the request. It does not store your data or apps.

items.title: Devices are doorways
items.description: Today, a phone. Later, smart glasses or headsets.

slide.footer: The substance of the assistant stays on your own computer.

beats.text: AI will clearly become more and more important. And there will be a large gap between people who can use AI well and people who cannot. I do not think that gap will be decided simply by who can rent a smarter model. It will be decided by who can nurture an AI assistant that understands them.

slide.quote: The gap will not come from renting a smarter model. It will come from {primary:nurturing an AI assistant that understands you}.
slide.author: Move to the side that can use AI well

beats.text: Please install MulmoClaude and start nurturing your own AI assistant. It is fine to begin with nothing. Start the accumulation on your own computer.

slide.title: Nurture your own **AI Assistant**
slide.subtitle: Start the accumulation on {primary:your own computer}.
